item.title: STARFlow2: Bridging Language Models and Normalizing Flows for Unified Multimodal Generation
item.description: Deep generative models have advanced rapidly across text and vision, motivating unified multimodal systems that can understand, reason over, and generate interleaved text-image sequences. Most existing approaches combine autoregressive l...

item.title: Flow-OPD: On-Policy Distillation for Flow Matching Models
item.description: Existing Flow Matching (FM) text-to-image models suffer from two critical bottlenecks under multi-task alignment: the reward sparsity induced by scalar-valued rewards, and the gradient interference arising from jointly optimizing heterog...

item.title: CASCADE: Case-Based Continual Adaptation for Large Language Models During Deployment
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) have become a central foundation of modern artificial intelligence, yet their lifecycle remains constrained by a rigid separation between training and deployment, after which learning effectively ceases. This...

item.title: LLMs Improving LLMs: Agentic Discovery for Test-Time Scaling
item.description: Test-time scaling (TTS) has become an effective approach for improving large language model performance by allocating additional computation during inference. However, existing TTS strategies are largely hand-crafted: researchers manuall...

item.title: HyperEyes: Dual-Grained Efficiency-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Parallel Multimodal Search Agents
item.description: Existing multimodal search agents process target entities sequentially, issuing one tool call per entity and accumulating redundant interaction rounds whenever a query decomposes into independent sub-retrievals. We argue that effective m...

item.title: A^2RD: Agentic Autoregressive Diffusion for Long Video Consistency
item.description: Synthesizing consistent and coherent long video remains a fundamental challenge. Existing methods suffer from semantic drift and narrative collapse over long horizons. We present A^2RD, an Agentic Auto-Regressive Diffusion architecture t...

item.title: UniSD: Towards a Unified Self-Distillation Framework for Large Language Models
item.description: Self-distillation (SD) offers a promising path for adapting large language models (LLMs) without relying on stronger external teachers. However, SD in autoregressive LLMs remains challenging because self-generated trajectories are free-f...

item.title: Normalizing Trajectory Models
item.description: Diffusion-based models decompose sampling into many small Gaussian denoising steps -- an assumption that breaks down when generation is compressed to a few coarse transitions. Existing few-step methods address this through distillation,...

item.title: InterLV-Search: Benchmarking Interleaved Multimodal Agentic Search
item.description: Existing benchmarks for multimodal agentic search evaluate multimodal search and visual browsing, but visual evidence is either confined to the input or treated as an answer endpoint rather than part of an interleaved search trajectory....

item.title: Sparse Autoencoders as Plug-and-Play Firewalls for Adversarial Attack Detection in VLMs
item.description: Vision-language models (VLMs) have advanced rapidly and are increasingly deployed in real-world applications, especially with the rise of agent-based systems. However, their safety has received relatively limited attention. Even the late...

item.title: MACE-Dance: Motion-Appearance Cascaded Experts for Music-Driven Dance Video Generation
item.description: With the rise of online dance-video platforms and rapid advances in AI-generated content (AIGC), music-driven dance generation has emerged as a compelling research direction. Despite substantial progress in related domains such as music-...

item.title: Rubric-based On-policy Distillation
item.description: On-policy distillation (OPD) is a powerful paradigm for model alignment, yet its reliance on teacher logits restricts its application to white-box scenarios. We contend that structured semantic rubrics can serve as a scalable alternative...

item.title: Anisotropic Modality Align
item.description: Training multimodal large language models has long been limited by the scarcity of high-quality paired multimodal data. Recent studies show that the shared representation space of pretrained multimodal contrastive models can serve as a b...

item.title: TextLDM: Language Modeling with Continuous Latent Diffusion
item.description: Diffusion Transformers (DiT) trained with flow matching in a VAE latent space have unified visual generation across images and videos. A natural next step toward a single architecture for both generation (visual synthesis) and understand...

item.title: DecodingTrust-Agent Platform (DTap): A Controllable and Interactive Red-Teaming Platform for AI Agents
item.description: AI agents are increasingly deployed across diverse domains to automate complex workflows through long-horizon and high-stakes action executions. Due to their high capability and flexibility, such agents raise significant security and saf...

item.title: AEM: Adaptive Entropy Modulation for Multi-Turn Agentic Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Reinforcement learning (RL) has substantially improved the ability of large language model (LLM) agents to interact with environments and solve multi-turn tasks. However, effective agentic RL remains challenging: sparse outcome-only rewa...

item.title: From Storage to Experience: A Survey on the Evolution of LLM Agent Memory Mechanisms
item.description: Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents have fundamentally reshaped artificial intelligence by integrating external tools and planning capabilities. While memory mechanisms have emerged as the architectural cornerstone of these systems,...

item.title: LiVeAction: a Lightweight, Versatile, and Asymmetric Neural Codec Design for Real-time Operation
item.description: Modern sensors generate rich, high-fidelity data, yet applications operating on wearable or remote sensing devices remain constrained by bandwidth and power budgets. Standardized codecs such as JPEG and MPEG achieve efficient trade-offs...

item.title: MDN: Parallelizing Stepwise Momentum for Delta Linear Attention
item.description: Linear Attention (LA) offers a promising paradigm for scaling large language models (LLMs) to long sequences by avoiding the quadratic complexity of self-attention. Recent LA models such as Mamba2 and GDN interpret linear recurrences as...

item.title: PrefixGuard: From LLM-Agent Traces to Online Failure-Warning Monitors
item.description: Large language model (LLM) agents now execute long, tool-using tasks where final outcome checks can arrive too late for intervention. Online warning requires lightweight prefix monitors over heterogeneous traces, but hand-authored event...

item.title: Q-RAG: Long Context Multi-step Retrieval via Value-based Embedder Training
item.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) methods enhance LLM performance by efficiently filtering relevant context for LLMs, reducing hallucinations and inference cost. However, most existing RAG methods focus on single-step retrieval, which...

item.title: What Matters for Diffusion-Friendly Latent Manifold? Prior-Aligned Autoencoders for Latent Diffusion
item.description: Tokenizers are a crucial component of latent diffusion models, as they define the latent space in which diffusion models operate. However, existing tokenizers are primarily designed to improve reconstruction fidelity or inherit pretraine...

item.title: ModelLens: Finding the Best for Your Task from Myriads of Models
item.description: The open-source model ecosystem now contains hundreds of thousands of pretrained models, yet picking the best model for a new dataset is increasingly infeasible: new models and unbenchmarked datasets emerge continuously, leaving practiti...

item.title: IntentGrasp: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Intent Understanding
item.description: Accurately understanding the intent behind speech, conversation, and writing is crucial to the development of helpful Large Language Model (LLM) assistants. This paper introduces IntentGrasp, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating the...

item.title: SkCC: Portable and Secure Skill Compilation for Cross-Framework LLM Agents
item.description: LLM-Agents have evolved into autonomous systems for complex task execution, with the SKILL.md specification emerging as a de facto standard for encapsulating agent capabilities. However, a critical bottleneck remains: different agent fra...

item.title: MLS-Bench: A Holistic and Rigorous Assessment of AI Systems on Building Better AI
item.description: Modern AI progress has been driven by ML methods that are generalizable across settings and scalable to larger regimes. As large language models demonstrate advanced capabilities in reasoning, coding, and engineering tasks, it is increas...

item.title: DiffRetriever: Parallel Representative Tokens for Retrieval with Diffusion Language Models
item.description: PromptReps showed that an autoregressive language model can be used directly as a retriever by prompting it to generate dense and sparse representations of a query or passage. Extending this to multiple representatives is inefficient for...

item.title: SpecBlock: Block-Iterative Speculative Decoding with Dynamic Tree Drafting
item.description: Speculative decoding accelerates LLM inference by drafting a tree of candidate continuations and verifying it in one target forward. Existing drafters fall into two camps with opposite weaknesses. Autoregressive drafters such as EAGLE-3...

item.title: PACEvolve++: Improving Test-time Learning for Evolutionary Search Agents
item.description: Large language models have become drivers of evolutionary search, but most systems rely on a fixed, prompt-elicited policy to sample next candidates. This limits adaptation in practical engineering and research tasks, where evaluations a...

item.title: Rethinking RL for LLM Reasoning: It's Sparse Policy Selection, Not Capability Learning
item.description: Reinforcement learning has become the standard for improving reasoning in large language models, yet evidence increasingly suggests that RL does not teach new strategies; it redistributes probability mass over solutions the base model al...

item.title: Steering Visual Generation in Unified Multimodal Models with Understanding Supervision
item.description: Unified multimodal models are envisioned to bridge the gap between understanding and generation. Yet, to achieve competitive performance, state-of-the-art models adopt largely decoupled understanding and generation components. This desig...

item.title: Learning Visual Feature-Based World Models via Residual Latent Action
item.description: World models predict future transitions from observations and actions. Existing works predominantly focus on image generation only. Visual feature-based world models, on the other hand, predict future visual features instead of raw video...

item.title: BalCapRL: A Balanced Framework for RL-Based MLLM Image Captioning
item.description: Image captioning is one of the most fundamental tasks in computer vision. Owing to its open-ended nature, it has received significant attention in the era of multimodal large language models (MLLMs). In pursuit of ever more detailed and...

item.title: CPCANet: Deep Unfolding Common Principal Component Analysis for Domain Generalization
item.description: Domain Generalization (DG) aims to learn representations that remain robust under out-of-distribution (OOD) shifts and generalize effectively to unseen target domains. While recent invariant learning strategies and architectural advances...

item.title: Listwise Policy Optimization: Group-based RLVR as Target-Projection on the LLM Response Simplex
item.description: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has become a standard approach for large language models (LLMs) post-training to incentivize reasoning capacity. Among existing recipes, group-based policy gradient is prevalent, whic...

item.title: UniPrefill: Universal Long-Context Prefill Acceleration via Block-wise Dynamic Sparsification
item.description: As large language models (LLMs) continue to advance rapidly, they are becoming increasingly capable while simultaneously demanding ever-longer context lengths. To improve the inference efficiency of long-context processing, several novel...

item.title: 4DThinker: Thinking with 4D Imagery for Dynamic Spatial Understanding
item.description: Dynamic spatial reasoning from monocular video is essential for bridging visual intelligence and the physical world, yet remains challenging for vision-language models (VLMs). Prior approaches either verbalize spatial-temporal reasoning...

item.title: SCOPE: Structured Decomposition and Conditional Skill Orchestration for Complex Image Generation
item.description: While text-to-image models have made strong progress in visual fidelity, faithfully realizing complex visual intents remains challenging because many requirements must be tracked across grounding, generation, and verification. We refer t...

item.title: Fast Byte Latent Transformer
item.description: Recent byte-level language models (LMs) match the performance of token-level models without relying on subword vocabularies, yet their utility is limited by slow, byte-by-byte autoregressive generation. We address this bottleneck in the...

item.title: Discovering Reinforcement Learning Interfaces with Large Language Models
item.description: Reinforcement learning systems rely on environment interfaces that specify observations and reward functions, yet constructing these interfaces for new tasks often requires substantial manual effort. While recent work has automated rewar...

item.title: R^3-SQL: Ranking Reward and Resampling for Text-to-SQL
item.description: Modern Text-to-SQL systems generate multiple candidate SQL queries and rank them to judge a final prediction. However, existing methods face two limitations. First, they often score functionally equivalent SQL queries inconsistently desp...

item.title: Shallow Prefill, Deep Decoding: Efficient Long-Context Inference via Layer-Asymmetric KV Visibility
item.description: Long-context inference in decoder-only language models is costly because long prompts are processed during Prefill, cached at every layer, and repeatedly attended to during autoregressive Decode. We introduce Shallow Prefill, dEEp Decode...

item.title: Trajectory as the Teacher: Few-Step Discrete Flow Matching via Energy-Navigated Distillation
item.description: Discrete flow matching generates text by iteratively transforming noise tokens into coherent language, but may require hundreds of forward passes. Distillation uses the multi-step trajectory to train a student to reproduce the process in...

item.title: Mean Mode Screaming: Mean--Variance Split Residuals for 1000-Layer Diffusion Transformers
item.description: Scaling Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) to hundreds of layers introduces a structural vulnerability: networks can enter a silent, mean-dominated collapse state that homogenizes token representations and suppresses centered variation. Throu...

item.title: HumanNet: Scaling Human-centric Video Learning to One Million Hours
item.description: Progress in embodied intelligence increasingly depends on scalable data infrastructure. While vision and language have scaled with internet corpora, learning physical interaction remains constrained by the lack of large, diverse, and ric...

item.title: Beyond Retrieval: A Multitask Benchmark and Model for Code Search
item.description: Code search has usually been evaluated as first-stage retrieval, even though production systems rely on broader pipelines with reranking and developer-style queries. Existing benchmarks also suffer from data contamination, label noise, a...

item.title: MatryoshkaLoRA: Learning Accurate Hierarchical Low-Rank Representations for LLM Fine-Tuning
item.description: With the rise in scale for deep learning models to billions of parameters, the computational cost of fine-tuning remains a significant barrier to deployment. While Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) has become the standard for parameter-efficien...

item.title: MISA: Mixture of Indexer Sparse Attention for Long-Context LLM Inference
item.description: DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA) sets the state of the art for fine-grained inference-time sparse attention by introducing a learned token-wise indexer that scores every prefix token and selects the most relevant ones for the main attenti...

item.title: Scaling Continual Learning to 300+ Tasks with Bi-Level Routing Mixture-of-Experts
item.description: Continual learning, especially class-incremental learning (CIL), on the basis of a pre-trained model (PTM) has garnered substantial research interest in recent years. However, how to effectively learn both discriminative and comprehensiv...

item.title: Who Prices Cognitive Labor in the Age of Agents? Compute-Anchored Wages
item.description: A natural intuition about the economics of AI agents is that, because agents can be replicated at very low marginal cost, agent labor may be supplied highly elastically, placing downward pressure on cognitive-labor wages when it closely...
